mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month expansion in industrial production signals rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0%, restrictive monetary policy elevates discount rates and debt-service costs, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS > 3.0% regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or are positive in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in retail sales and housing starts indicates dual-engine consumer and residential investment demand that amplifies earnings growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal from RSAFS and HOUST jointly does not outperform the best single-proxy signal by more than 50 basis points annualized (F-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a rapid two-month increase in the unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration that precedes consumption contractions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following UNRATE spikes in late-cycle regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or exhibit positive returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation signals impending monetary tightening and margin compression, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice forward earnings.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following high CPIAUCSL prints are not significantly negative (Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth fails to exhibit positive correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal contracting manufacturing activity and supply-side deterioration, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following two-month INDPRO declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of restrictive federal funds rates above 3.0% and rising unemployment signals a policy-induced hard landing that simultaneously compresses valuations and earnings, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 3.0% and UNRATE rises > 0.1pp in 1 month
regime_hint: restrictive policy with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS > 3.0% and rising UNRATE does not produce significantly worse 60-day returns than either condition alone (Chow test p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.
